plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate extreme risk.
Reduce exposure to semiconductors and concentrated tech names.
Increase allocation to income-generating ETFs for stability.
Add a high-quality, strong-scoring tech name for diversification.
Significantly reduce broad index exposure to manage concentration.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with lower expected returns.
conservative.read: This plan aims for ~1.5% monthly growth by cutting high-risk assets. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns, but also reduced upside potential.

plan: Reduce exposure to leveraged ETFs and semiconductors.
Trim the largest broad index holding to reduce concentration.
Increase allocation to income-generating ETFs for stability.
Add a high-quality tech name for diversification and growth.
Introduce a new strong-setup tech name for potential upside.
This plan balances risk reduction with growth opportunities.
balanced.read: This plan targets ~2.5% monthly growth with a risk-first approach. It reduces concentration and leverage while adding quality growth and income.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged semiconductor ETFs.
Add to existing semiconductor holding for sector concentration.
Reduce broad index and income ETF exposure.
Add top-scoring tech names with high risk/reward.
Maintain a small cash buffer for opportunistic entries.
This plan aggressively pursues growth through leverage and concentration.
aggressive.read: This plan targets ~4% monthly growth by leaning into leverage and concentrated high-conviction setups. Expect significantly higher volatility and potential for large drawdowns.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and volatile growth tech.
Eliminate semiconductor exposure entirely.
Maximize allocation to income-generating ETFs for yield.
Add an energy stock known for dividends.
Reduce broad index exposure to free up capital for income.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly income and capital preservation.
income.read: This plan aims for ~1% monthly growth focused on income generation. It minimizes volatility and drawdowns by holding stable income-producing assets.
